title: Hydra
description: Fast online password brute-forcing tool supporting 50+ protocols.
fullExplanation: Hydra (THC-Hydra) is a fast and flexible online password brute-forcing tool. Unlike Hashcat which cracks offline hashes, Hydra attacks live services over the network. It supports 50+ protocols including SSH, FTP, HTTP, HTTPS, SMB, RDP, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SMTP, and more. Parallelized attacks with configurable threads make it very fast.

commonCommands.description: FTP with username and password lists

keyFacts: 50+ supported protocols: SSH, FTP, HTTP, HTTPS, SMB, RDP, MySQL, SMTP...
Parallel attacks: default 16 threads — configurable with -t
Use -t 1-4 for services with lockout policies to avoid detection
Medusa: similar tool, sometimes faster for certain protocols
Patator: more flexible, module-based brute forcer
Always get written authorization before running Hydra
Account lockout policies will block Hydra — use password spraying instead
